key_insight: A zero-shot LLM-assisted workflow matched clinical trials for a metastatic pancreatic cancer case with performance close to a human-reviewed benchmark.
clinical_relevance: This suggests LLMs could help speed up trial matching for patients with limited treatment options, potentially reducing manual screening burden.
method: Compared zero-shot LLM-assisted trial eligibility results against a manually built human benchmark using ClinicalTrials.gov.
Calculated sensitivity, specificity, precision, and accuracy for the LLM-assisted matching process.
Performed qualitative content analysis of LLM reasoning text to examine mismatch patterns and likely error causes.
evidence_signal: In this single-patient case study, the LLM-assisted process achieved 81.1% sensitivity, 89.3% specificity, and 86.5% precision versus the human benchmark.
watch_item: Findings come from one real-world case, so broader reliability, workflow integration, and safety need validation before routine use.
paper_title: Assessment of Zero-Shot Large Language Model (LLM) Assisted Clinical Trial Matching Processes: A Metastatic Cancer Use Case
